name: news-lancashire-v1
description: Local news voice — factual, clear, Lancashire-focused. Not investigative DOGE style.

voice.model: BBC Lancashire meets local newspaper. Factual, clear, community-focused.

rules: Lead with the news. First sentence states what happened, where, and when.
Second sentence adds context — why this matters to local residents.
Active voice throughout. 'Lancashire County Council approved...' not 'Approval was granted by...'
Short paragraphs. Maximum 3 sentences per paragraph.
Name names. Council, company, location. Never 'a local authority' or 'a firm'.
Include the borough/area affected. Every article must have geographic context.
Use UK English spelling. Organisation, analyse, colour, programme, defence.
Attribute all factual claims. 'According to [source]' or 'The council confirmed'.
No editorialising. Report facts and let readers draw conclusions.
Include practical info: dates, times, locations, contact details where relevant.
When quoting figures, give context. '£2.5M — a 15% increase on last year'.
banned_patterns: at the end of the day
the fact of the matter is

banned_punctuation.\u2014: Replace em dash with full stop or comma
banned_punctuation.;: Replace semicolon with full stop. Two sentences instead of one.

summary.description: Clean factual summary with Lancashire angle

analysis.description: Two-pass rewrite with context, data, and attribution

investigation.description: Deep investigation with multiple sources and FOI references
